caveats: Only four of the six white-box bottleneck labels (hbm, tma, mma, mufu) are independently saturatable with the current workload model; l2 and smem bandwidths dominate the other memory stages in every tested configuration.

NCU SpeedOfLight reports only coarse compute/memory families. The fine label mapping back to mma/mufu and hbm/l2/smem/tma uses predictor per-stage times, so fine-label accuracy is not an independent hardware measurement.
All profiling used a single B200 GPU (cuda:0) with NCU 2025.4.1; multi-GPU and concurrency effects are not captured.
The experiment keeps the combined predictor's default calibration (hbm_factor=0.80, l2_factor=0.70, tma_factor=0.65, smem_factor=0.90, mma_efficiency=0.85, mufu_efficiency=0.85).
sections.claim_update: The selected hypothesis is partially supported: the white-box predictor correctly identifies compute-bound regimes (mma and mufu) but over-predicts memory/TMA dominance for several configurations that hardware counters measure as compute-bound.
sections.comparability: Ground truth is NCU SpeedOfLight compute/memory throughput on real B200 hardware. Predictions come from the combined white-box predictor with no runtime calibration; only bottleneck labels are compared.
sections.failure_mode: The model's TMA time is too large relative to MMA time for medium-size problems, causing three TMA-targeted configs to be labelled memory-bound by the model while NCU reports them as compute-bound. One HBM-targeted config is also misclassified.
sections.intervention: Selected 12 extreme FA4 configurations across hbm/tma/mma/mufu regimes, predicted bottleneck labels with the combined white-box predictor, and profiled each config with ncu --section SpeedOfLight on cuda:0.
sections.next_action: Proceed to isomer-deepsci-analysis to diagnose why the model over-weights TMA relative to MMA for medium-size configs and whether an overlap term or revised TMA bandwidth curve improves bottleneck classification.
sections.research_question: Can the FA4 B200 white-box predictor correctly predict the dominant bottleneck regime when configurations are pushed to saturate a specific hardware component?
sections.takeaway: The predictor is a useful bottleneck classifier for strongly compute-bound configs (mma/mufu) but currently misclassifies medium-size memory/TMA configs as memory-bound when hardware counters show they are compute-bound.

summary: Bottleneck-saturation experiment on B200: predictor is 100% accurate on mma/mufu regimes but 0% accurate on the tma regime and 67% on the hbm regime.
title: Experiment Result Summary: Bottleneck-Saturation Validation of the FA4 B200 Predictor
